fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Rhode Island Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Rhode Island - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity rhode island, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to rhode island, respiratory therapist compact rhode island, rhode island respiratory therapist endorsement application

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every two years

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Rhode Island.

steps.title: Create Account and Log In
steps.description: Register for a new user account on the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal (prc.health.ri.gov/PRC/login.aspx) and log in to begin.

steps.title: Start New Application
steps.description: Select 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' from the list of available application types within the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal and complete all required sections of the application.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documentation, including proof of your NBRC Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT) credential, official transcripts from your respiratory care program, and any other specified forms directly to the online portal.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee securely through the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Request Official Verifications
steps.description: Arrange for official verification of your NBRC RRT credential to be sent directly from the NBRC to the Rhode Island Department of Health.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application within the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Rhode Island respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Rhode Island have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Rhode Island. To work in Rhode Island, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Rhode Island?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Rhode Island while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Rhode Island
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Care Practitioner license in Rhode Island, you must submit your application electronically through the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal.

official_links.portal_name: Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal
official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the Rhode Island Department of Health Online Licensing Portal.
2. Click 'Register Here' to create a new user account.
3. Complete the registration form by providing personal details, creating a username, and setting a password.
4. Verify your email address if prompted.
5. Log in with your newly created credentials to access the application dashboard.
